title: enQase Achieves FIPS 140-3 Overall Level 1 Validation for Cryptographic Module - Quantum Computing Report
author: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
text:
enQase Achieves FIPS 140-3 Overall Level 1 Validation for Cryptographic Module

Quantum-safe security provider enQase has officially received FIPS 140-3 Certificate #5346 from the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP). Independently tested and verified by DEKRA Cybersecurity Certification Laboratory, the enQase FIPS 140-3 Cryptographic Module is now designated as an active, validated software cryptographic module with an Overall Level 1 validation. The milestone establishes a fully compliant, regulatory-approved cryptographic engine for enterprises, government agencies, and critical infrastructure providers transitioning to post-quantum cryptography (PQC).

Enabling Crypto-Agility for Regulated Sectors

FIPS 140-3 is the current U.S. and Canadian federal standard governing the security requirements for cryptographic modules used to protect sensitive, unclassified data. Securing this validation is an absolute commercial prerequisite for vendors deploying security architectures within regulated sectors, including federal defense departments, public utility operators, and financial service infrastructures.

The enQase module addresses the complex transition to quantum-safe primitives by functioning as a highly flexible, software-defined cryptographic engine:

Crypto-Agility Architecture: The platform is engineered to support seamless swaps of underlying cryptographic algorithms without requiring a complete overhaul of the host network infrastructure, servers, or edge applications.

Unified Quantum-Safe Platform: The cryptographic engine unifies classical cryptography, physics-based quantum hardware integrations, and an automated software-defined abstraction layer.

Verified Implementation Protection: Completing the CMVP pipeline verifies that the module’s logical boundaries, physical ports, and approved key generation techniques comply with strict federal requirements.

Backed by a portfolio of 15 U.S. and international patents, the newly validated cryptographic module will serve as the trusted compliance foundation for the full-stack enQase Platform. By validating its core software engine under the current FIPS 140-3 guidelines, enQase enables enterprise clients to begin deploying standards-based, quantum-resistant algorithms across data, network, and computing layers with audited assurance.

Verify the active regulatory listing directly via the NIST CMVP Certificate #5346 Database Entry here, and explore the corporate rollout announcement here.

July 16, 2026
